chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT LARKANA 2nd Constitutional Petition No.S-446 of 2024 ________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF HON’BLE JUDGE_________ 01. For orders on office objection “A” 02. For hearing of main case. 30-05-2025 Mr. Muhammad Ibrahim Lashari, advocate for the petitioner. Mr. Munwar Ali Abbassi, Assistant Advocate General, Sindh. -------- 1. Through this petition, the petitioner had challenged the orders passed by the Guardian and Wards Court and the appellate court regarding the custody of minors Muhammad Taha and baby Tahseen wherein the custody of minors was given to the respondent subject to a visiting right to the petitioner. Today the petitioner and respondent are present before the court and they submit that they have patched put the dispute amicably and they intend to reside again as wedded couple. The respondent submits that out of anger he had sent a divorce letter to the petitioner which was just to get the terms settled and reunite as a family and the said divorce letter was not issued with free will and consent. 2. The respondent had issued a divorce letter dated 03-04-2024 contending therein that he had divorced petitioner. This divorce would have been effective in in case given voluntarily. The respondent submits that divorce letter was not written by him and it was got printed from the stamp vendor. The perusal of divorce letter reflects that the divorce has not been given in accordance with the Sharia and it has not been pronounced in person to the petitioner, therefore, under the law it loses its importance and has got no evidentiary value.

The respondent submits that divorce letter was not written by him and it was got printed from the stamp vendor. The perusal of divorce letter reflects that the divorce has not been given in accordance with the Sharia and it has not been pronounced in person to the petitioner, therefore, under the law it loses its importance and has got no evidentiary value. The petitioner and respondent can unite as the respondent has admitted that he had issued the divorce letter to the petitioner out of anger. He further submits that he had not read the paper and asked his friend to send it to petitioner. 3. Since the parties have patched up the differences and they have united again, therefore, this petition has served its purpose and stands disposed of. JUDGE Asghar/P.A
